title: plan_mode unwrap_or_default on allowed_prompts silently accepts non-array shapes
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Perimeter Defense / Functional Correctness

## Location
src/tools/plan_mode.rs:18-22 (execute_exit_plan_mode)

## Violation
```rust
let allowed_prompts = args
    .get("allowed_prompts")
    .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
    .cloned()
    .unwrap_or_default();
```

If `allowed_prompts` is supplied but is not an array (e.g. the model returns a JSON object or a string), `as_array()` returns None, `cloned()` returns None, and `unwrap_or_default()` returns Vec::new(). The tool silently treats "malformed input" identically to "no input." The model gets no feedback that its argument was rejected. This is precisely the perimeter-defense violation in §3.4 — "Reject invalid inputs and throw immediate exceptions. Do not return default/null values to mask errors."

## Mandated refactor
Split the lookup:
```rust
let allowed_prompts = match args.get("allowed_prompts") {
    None => Vec::new(),
    Some(Value::Array(arr)) => arr.clone(),
    Some(other) => return (format!("allowed_prompts must be an array, got {}", other.type_name()), true),
};
```
The existing validation loop already returns is_error=true for malformed entries — be consistent and return is_error=true for a malformed container.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
